summary: Recent updates in the Web Speech area have integrated Web Speech technology—a tool that allows web applications to convert spoken language into text or respond to voice commands—more closely with the media-control infrastructure of the Firefox browser. This means that Web Speech can now act as an "uncontrollable receiver," enabling better interaction between voice commands and media playback features. This advancement is important as it enhances user experiences by making voice interactions more fluid and responsive, ultimately improving how we engage with multimedia content online.
